page.title: Cabopino Boat Rental for Sunset Cruise: Best Routes & Tips

data.meta_description: Book a Cabopino boat rental sunset cruise from Puerto Banús. Explore best routes, costs from €749/2h, and tips for an unforgettable evening on the Costa del Sol.
data.summary: Discover the best routes and tips for a Cabopino boat rental sunset cruise from Puerto Banús. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours, with all drinks and skipper included.
key_facts: Sunset cruises depart from Puerto Banús, just 10 minutes from Cabopino.
Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39.
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance.
Popular sunset routes: Cabopino, Cala del Faro, and Cala Cortés.
Boats accommodate 5 to 12 passengers depending on the model.
Best sunset timing: 30 minutes before official sunset – book accordingly.
Mangusta 80 (24m) includes a free jet ski for added fun.
Spanish IVA (21%) is included in all listed prices.

faq.q: What is the best time for a Cabopino sunset cruise?
faq.a: The best time is 30–45 minutes before official sunset, typically around 19:30–20:30 in summer and 17:30–18:30 in winter. We recommend booking a 2-hour slot to enjoy the golden hour and twilight over the Mediterranean.

faq.q: How much does a Cabopino boat rental sunset cruise cost?
faq.a: Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (up to 11 passengers). For larger groups, the Mangusta 80 (24m) costs €4,719 for a minimum 4-hour charter and includes a free jet ski. All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, and VAT.

faq.q: Which boats are available for a sunset cruise?
faq.a: We offer a range of boats from our fleet in Puerto Banús, including the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax), Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax), Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax), and smaller options like the Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m, 5 pax). All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: What is included in a sunset cruise charter?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras – just show up and enjoy.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food or drinks?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The boat is equipped with a cooler and basic amenities. Many guests bring a picnic or tapas to enjoy as the sun sets over the sea.

faq.q: What are the best sunset routes from Cabopino?
faq.a: Popular routes include heading east towards Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés for dramatic cliff views, or west towards Estepona and Sotogrande for a longer cruise. The water near Cabopino is calm, ideal for swimming stops during golden hour.

faq.q: Is it possible to swim during a sunset cruise?
faq.a: Absolutely. The skipper will anchor in a sheltered bay like Cala Cortés or near Cabopino beach, where you can swim in the warm Mediterranean waters as the sun sets. Life jackets and safety equipment are provided.

faq.q: How do I book a Cabopino sunset cruise?
faq.a: You can book directly via WhatsApp or our website. For boats not listed with fixed pricing, contact us for a custom quote. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during peak summer months.

data.body_html:
Imagine gliding across the Mediterranean as the sky turns shades of orange and pink, with the Sierra Blanca mountains as your backdrop. A Cabopino boat rental sunset cruise offers exactly that – a private, unforgettable evening on the water. Departing from Puerto Banús, just 10 minutes from Cabopino, our fleet of modern motor yachts provides the perfect setting for a sunset experience. Whether you're celebrating a special occasion or simply unwinding after a day on the Costa del Sol, here's everything you need to know about routes, costs, and tips.

Why Choose a Cabopino Sunset Cruise?

Cabopino is one of the Costa del Sol's hidden gems – a quiet marina with a sandy beach and dunes, far less crowded than Puerto Banús. A sunset cruise from here offers stunning views of the coastline, from the cliffs of Cala del Faro to the golden sands of Marbella. With our boats departing from Puerto Banús, you're just a short sail away from these serene waters. The calm sea conditions in the evening make for a smooth ride, ideal for families, couples, or groups of friends.

Best Routes for a Sunset Cruise

Our skippers know the local waters intimately. Here are three recommended routes for your Cabopino sunset cruise:

  • Cabopino to Cala del Faro (East): A 30-minute cruise eastwards takes you past the dunes of Cabopino to the dramatic cliffs of Cala del Faro. Anchor in the bay for a swim as the sun dips below the horizon.
  • Cabopino to Cala Cortés (East): Continue further east to Cala Cortés, a secluded cove with crystal-clear water. This route offers uninterrupted sunset views and is perfect for a romantic evening.
  • Cabopino to Estepona (West): For a longer cruise, head west towards Estepona. You'll pass the Golden Mile and Marbella Marina, with the sunset painting the sky behind La Concha mountain.

Boat Options and Pricing

We offer several boats suitable for sunset cruises. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on an Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) – both include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. For larger groups, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) starts at €4,719 for 4 hours and includes a free jet ski. Other boats like the Fairline Targa 12m (12m, 10 pax) or Bandido (11.5m, 8 pax) are available on request via WhatsApp. All prices include Spanish IVA (21%).

What's Included in Your Charter

Every sunset cruise comes with a full package: a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and safety equipment. You're free to bring your own food – many guests enjoy a picnic of local jamón, cheese, and olives as the sun sets. The skipper will handle navigation and anchoring, so you can relax completely.

Tips for an Unforgettable Sunset Cruise

  • Book early: Summer evenings are popular – reserve at least 48 hours in advance.
  • Check sunset time: Official sunset varies from 18:30 in winter to 21:30 in summer. Plan to depart 30 minutes before.
  • Dress comfortably: Evenings can be cooler on the water – bring a light jacket or sweater.
  • Bring a camera: The light is magical, especially near Cabopino's dunes and cliffs.
  • Ask about jet skis: On Mangusta 80 charters, a jet ski is included – perfect for sunset spins.

Why Book with Boat Rental Marbella?

We are not an affiliate – every boat in our fleet is available for direct booking. Our team is based in Puerto Banús and knows the best spots for sunset views. With transparent pricing, no hidden fees, and a fleet ranging from 6.4m speedboats to 29m luxury yachts, we have the right boat for your evening. Explore our full range of boat rentals in Marbella to find your perfect sunset vessel.

Frequently asked questions

What is the best time for a Cabopino sunset cruise?

The best time is 30–45 minutes before official sunset, typically around 19:30–20:30 in summer and 17:30–18:30 in winter. We recommend booking a 2-hour slot to enjoy the golden hour and twilight over the Mediterranean.

How much does a Cabopino boat rental sunset cruise cost?

Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (up to 11 passengers). For larger groups, the Mangusta 80 (24m) costs €4,719 for a minimum 4-hour charter and includes a free jet ski. All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, and VAT.

Which boats are available for a sunset cruise?

We offer a range of boats from our fleet in Puerto Banús, including the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax), Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax), Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax), and smaller options like the Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m, 5 pax). All depart from Puerto Banús.

What is included in a sunset cruise charter?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden extras – just show up and enjoy.

Can we bring our own food or drinks?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. The boat is equipped with a cooler and basic amenities. Many guests bring a picnic or tapas to enjoy as the sun sets over the sea.

What are the best sunset routes from Cabopino?

Popular routes include heading east towards Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés for dramatic cliff views, or west towards Estepona and Sotogrande for a longer cruise. The water near Cabopino is calm, ideal for swimming stops during golden hour.

Is it possible to swim during a sunset cruise?

Absolutely. The skipper will anchor in a sheltered bay like Cala Cortés or near Cabopino beach, where you can swim in the warm Mediterranean waters as the sun sets. Life jackets and safety equipment are provided.

How do I book a Cabopino sunset cruise?

You can book directly via WhatsApp or our website. For boats not listed with fixed pricing, contact us for a custom quote. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during peak summer months.
